bull.b: A cash balance near $184M and management's runway guidance into H2 2027 materially reduce near-term refinancing risk and fund staged commercialization plus a Phase III trial, providing time to demonstrate commercial traction or secure repeatable revenue before capital markets are needed.

Fast deployment of a national sales force and launch within months of approval shows operational capability to commercialize specialty cardiovascular drugs. This organizational execution supports sustained market access efforts and scales promotional reach as payer coverage and patient activation expand.

Securing a national PBM placement early materially improves patient access and reimbursement predictability. Coverage by Express Scripts validates formulary positioning and materially reduces structural barriers to uptake, enabling more efficient sales effort and long-term prescription growth as additional payers are a

bear.b: Debt-to-equity near 3.8x for a loss-making biotech elevates refinancing and covenant risk and increases future dilution if equity financings are required. Over multiple quarters this leverage can constrain strategic flexibility, raise interest burden and complicate funding for organic growth or trials.

Persistent negative operating and free cash flow (~-$51M TTM) underscores dependence on external capital to fund operations and launches. Continued quarterly cash burn will erode runway absent accelerating product revenue or new financing, pressuring management to prioritize capital-efficient growth or raise dilutive f

bear.t: Very Limited Product Revenue / Nascent Refill Dynamics
bear.b: Extremely low initial product revenue and early single-script behavior mean refill and repeat-use economics are unproven. Sustainable revenues require broader payer coverage, patient activation and repeat prescriptions; until these structural adoption dynamics are established, margin leverage from launch investments wi
